title: Dicranopteris scalpturata
intro: Dicranopteris scalpturata, also known as the "queens fern" or "diluwang," is a species of fern that belongs to the family Gleicheniaceae, native to tropical Asia. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dicranopteris scalpturata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Dicranopteris scalpturata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a paste or ointment, to take advantage of its health benefits.

The family Gleicheniaceae is a group of ferns that are commonly found in tropical regions.
